title: The State Hermitage Russia's Amazing Museum
titleZh: 俄罗斯令人惊叹的埃尔米塔日博物馆

q.en: In which city is the State Hermitage Museum located?
q.zh: 国立埃尔米塔日博物馆位于哪座城市？

explain.en: The book begins: 'Welcome to the State Hermitage Museum in St. Petersburg, Russia.'
explain.zh: 书中开头写道：'欢迎来到俄罗斯圣彼得堡的国立埃尔米塔日博物馆。'

q.en: Who started the Hermitage's art collection?
q.zh: 谁开创了埃尔米塔日的艺术收藏？

explain.en: The book says: 'The Hermitage began as the art collection of Catherine the Great. In 1764, she bought 225 paintings by Dutch and Flemish masters.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'埃尔米塔日始于叶卡捷琳娜大帝的艺术收藏。1764年，她购买了225幅荷兰和佛兰德斯大师的画作。'

q.en: About how many works of art are in the Hermitage's collection?
q.zh: 埃尔米塔日的收藏约有艺术品多少件？

explain.en: The narrator says the museum has 'more than three million works of art.'
explain.zh: 叙述者说博物馆拥有'超过三百万件艺术品'。

q.en: What animals patrol the Hermitage to keep mice from harming the collections?
q.zh: 什么动物在埃尔米塔日巡逻，以防老鼠破坏藏品？

explain.en: The book says cats 'were brought in to patrol the palace to keep mice from destroying the collections. About fifty cats still patrol the Hermitage.'
explain.zh: 书中说猫'被带进宫殿巡逻，以防老鼠破坏藏品。约五十只猫仍在埃尔米塔日巡逻。'

q.en: Which French artist has a whole room devoted to his work at the Hermitage?
q.zh: 哪位法国艺术家在埃尔米塔日拥有一整间专门展示其作品的厅？

explain.en: The narrator says: 'My favorite paintings are in the museum's collection of works by the French artist, Gauguin. He has a whole room devoted to his work.'
explain.zh: 叙述者说：'我最喜欢的画作属于法国艺术家高更的馆藏。他有一整间专门展示其作品的厅。'

q.en: If a visitor spent two minutes looking at each work, about how long would it take to see the whole collection?
q.zh: 如果游客每件作品看两分钟，看完整个收藏大约需要多久？

explain.en: The book says: 'If you spent only two minutes looking at each work of art... it would take you more than forty-three years to see the entire collection.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'若你在开放时间内每件作品只看两分钟……要看完整个收藏需超过四十三年。'

q.en: Which disaster happened in 1941 during the Second World War?
q.zh: 第二次世界大战期间，1941年发生了哪场灾难？

options.en: German troops attacked and the city was besieged for 900 days
options.zh: 德军进攻，城市被围困900天

options.en: A fire at the Winter Palace

explain.en: The book says: 'My mother survived the third disaster in 1941. German troops attacked... The Germans cut off St. Petersburg (then called Leningrad) for 900 days.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'我母亲熬过了1941年的第三次灾难。德军进攻……德军将圣彼得堡（当时称列宁格勒）围困了900天。'

q.en: Who opened the Hermitage to the public, and in what year?
q.zh: 谁在何时向公众开放了埃尔米塔日？

explain.en: The book says: 'Russian emperor Nicholas I opened the museum to the public in 1852. He built a new building and called it the New Hermitage.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'俄罗斯皇帝尼古拉一世于1852年向公众开放博物馆。他建了一座新楼，称为新埃尔米塔日。'

q.en: During World War II, where were two special trains filled with art sent to protect it?
q.zh: 二战期间，两列满载艺术品的专列被送往何处以保护藏品？

explain.en: The book says: 'Two special trains filled with art were sent to a remote part of Russia in the Ural Mountains.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'两列满载艺术品的专列被送往俄罗斯偏远地区的乌拉尔山脉。'

q.en: What does the narrator Pavel say the museum itself feels like when he walks into Pavilion Hall?
q.zh: 叙述者帕维尔说，当他走进展馆大厅时，博物馆本身像什么？

explain.en: The book says: 'I feel as if I'm inside a wedding cake whenever I walk into Pavilion Hall.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'每当我走进展馆大厅，都觉得自己仿佛置身一个婚礼蛋糕之中。'

prompt.en: The Hermitage survived three disasters over its history. Why do you think people worked so hard to save the art collection? Write your answer.
prompt.zh: 埃尔米塔日博物馆在历史上挺过了三次灾难。你认为人们为何如此努力地保护艺术收藏？写下你的答案。

en: Name at least one of the disasters from the book
Explain why saving art might matter to people
Use a detail such as the cats, the trains to the Urals, or the 900-day siege
zh: 至少说出书中一次灾难
解释保护艺术对人们为何重要
运用如猫、运往乌拉尔的专列或900天围困等细节
